files.complete_text:
LESSON II.
Work and Business
(Required)
Second Week in February.

TEXT: "History of the Church," Vol. IV, page 231; Doc. and Cov. sec. 42:42; 75:3; 68:30-32; 88:124; 119:4.

AIM: To teach the dignity of labor and the necessity of industry.

The Dignity of Labor.

The gospel of Jesus Christ is a gospel of work. It teaches the dignity of labor and the necessity of industry. It is opposed to idleness in every form. "Thou shalt not be idle," is one of its fundamental commandments. "The idler shall not have place in the Church except he repent and mend his ways." These are the words of modern revelation.

The gospel teaches that honest labor, of whatever kind, is honorable. There is no disgrace in any honest calling. The man who works with his hands is just as honorable as the man who works with his brain, provided both are honest and industrious.

Jesus himself was a carpenter. Paul was a tentmaker. The Savior chose his apostles from among the working classes—fishermen, publicans, and others who earned their bread by honest toil.

The Church has always taught the dignity of labor. The Prophet Joseph Smith worked with his hands. Brigham Young was a carpenter and joiner. Most of the early leaders of the Church were working men.

The gospel teaches that every man should have some useful occupation. "Every man should be diligent in a calling." No one should be a burden upon others if he can help it. "He who will not work, neither shall he eat."

The Church has always encouraged its members to be industrious and self-supporting. It has established industries and encouraged the development of the resources of the country. It has taught the people to be producers as well as consumers.

The gospel teaches that work is a blessing, not a curse. It is through work that man develops his powers and becomes like his Creator. "My Father worketh hitherto, and I work," said the Savior.

QUESTIONS.

1. What does the gospel teach concerning work?
2. What does it teach concerning idleness?
3. What was the occupation of Jesus?
4. What was Paul's trade?
5. From what class did the Savior choose his apostles?
6. What has the Church always taught concerning labor?
7. What occupations did Joseph Smith and Brigham Young follow?
8. What does the gospel teach concerning having a useful occupation?
9. How has the Church encouraged industry?
10. What does the gospel teach concerning the nature of work?
files.title: LESSON II. Work and Business
